data.title: Is It Too Late To Consider ABB (SWX:ABBN) After Its Strong Multi‑Year Share Price Run?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether ABB is still fairly priced after a strong run, this article will walk through what the current share price might be implying about value.
  • ABB's share price closed at CHF 69.80, with returns of 1.7% over 7 days, 13.3% over 30 days, 14.0% year to date, 34.5% over 1 year, 139.7% over 3 years and 215.6% over 5 years. This raises the question of what is already reflected in the price.
  • Recent news around ABB has focused on its position as a global industrial technology group. It highlights ABB's role in areas such as electrification, automation and robotics, which often attracts attention when investors reassess long term industrial themes. These developments help frame how the market might be thinking about ABB's future cash flows and risks, even without specific short term catalysts.
  • On our valuation checks, ABB records a value score of 1 out of 6. We will next walk through the main valuation approaches and then finish by looking at a more rounded way to think about what that score really means.

ABB scores just 1/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: ABB Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

The Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company could be worth today by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back to a single present value figure.

For ABB, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about US$4.6b. Analysts provide explicit forecasts for the next few years, and Simply Wall St then extrapolates those further out, with projected free cash flow reaching US$7.6b by 2030. Each of the annual free cash flows from 2026 to 2035 is discounted back to today and summed to get an overall equity value.

This process results in an estimated intrinsic value of CHF 62.28 per share, compared with the recent share price of CHF 69.80. On this model, ABB screens as about 12.1% overvalued at current levels.

Result: OVERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests ABB may be overvalued by 12.1%. Discover 225 high quality undervalued stocks or create your own screener to find better value opportunities.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for ABB.

Approach 2: ABB Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company like ABB, the P/E ratio is a useful shorthand for how much investors are currently paying for each unit of earnings. It links directly to what you receive as an owner, the earnings that can eventually be paid out or reinvested.

What counts as a “normal” or “fair” P/E depends on how the market views a company’s growth prospects and risk. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can justify a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk usually points to a lower P/E.

ABB currently trades on a P/E of 36.04x. That sits above both the Electrical industry average of 30.75x and the peer average of 30.71x, which suggests investors are paying a higher earnings multiple than for many sector peers.

Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for ABB is 40.48x. This is a proprietary metric that estimates what P/E might be appropriate given factors such as ABB’s earnings profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and risk characteristics. It moves beyond simple peer or sector comparisons by tailoring the multiple to the company’s own fundamentals.

Comparing the Fair Ratio of 40.48x with the current P/E of 36.04x, ABB screens as UNDERVALUED on this approach.

Result: UNDERVALUED

data.title: Teva’s Duvakitug Data Puts Ulcerative Colitis And Crohn’s Bet In Focus
data.description:
• Teva Pharmaceutical Industries (NYSE:TEVA) and Sanofi reported positive long term results from the RELIEVE UCCD extension study of duvakitug in ulcerative colitis and Crohn’s disease.
  • The TL1A antibody maintained efficacy and safety over 44 weeks in patients with inflammatory bowel disease.
  • The partners plan to advance duvakitug into phase III trials, targeting a potential best in class therapy in this treatment area.

For you as an investor following NYSE:TEVA, this update sits at the intersection of specialty medicine and a large chronic disease category. Teva remains widely known for its generics business, but its work in complex biologics and immunology is an important piece of how it positions itself in areas like inflammatory bowel disease.

The phase III decision puts timelines, trial design, and regulatory milestones in sharper focus for anyone tracking Teva’s pipeline. As more details emerge on enrollment, endpoints, and partner responsibilities, it will be easier to evaluate how duvakitug fits into the broader treatment field and Teva’s mix of branded and generic products.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Teva Pharmaceutical Industries by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Teva Pharmaceutical Industries.

3 things going right for Teva Pharmaceutical Industries that this headline doesn't cover.

For Teva, duvakitug sits right in the middle of its push to build a higher margin branded portfolio alongside the generics engine. Ulcerative colitis and Crohn’s disease are large, chronic markets already served by players such as AbbVie, Johnson & Johnson, and Takeda, so long term phase IIb data that hold up over 44 weeks matter for competitive positioning. The fact that Teva and Sanofi are moving quickly into phase III, supported by dedicated investor events and conference presentations, signals that management sees this as a program that could eventually matter for revenue mix if later stage trials read out well and regulators approve the drug.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Teva Pharmaceutical Industries Narrative

  • The progress with duvakitug supports the existing narrative that Teva’s R&D pipeline in large chronic conditions can complement brands like AUSTEDO, AJOVY, UZEDY, and the olanzapine LAI opportunity.
  • At the same time, heavier R&D commitments for phase III trials could test the narrative that Teva can expand its branded portfolio while still freeing up cash for debt reduction.
  • The potential role of Sanofi as a commercial partner in inflammatory bowel disease is not fully reflected in the narrative’s focus on Teva’s internal brands and biosimilars, which could influence how value is shared if duvakitug succeeds.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Teva Pharmaceutical Industries to help decide what it is worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Analysts have flagged Teva’s debt as a key risk, and funding large phase III programs for duvakitug could keep pressure on cash flow if costs rise or timelines extend.
  • ⚠️ The inflammatory bowel disease market is crowded, so even with positive data Teva and Sanofi still face competitive risk from established biologics and newer mechanisms.
  • 🎁 Duvakitug gives Teva a chance to add another potential high value biologic to its branded portfolio, which could help reduce reliance on a small group of existing drugs if it eventually reaches the market.
  • 🎁 The long term efficacy and safety profile reported for duvakitug may support a differentiated positioning within TL1A therapies, which could appeal to specialists if phase III outcomes are consistent.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, watch for more detail on duvakitug’s phase III trial design, including patient numbers, endpoints, and how Teva and Sanofi plan to share development and commercial responsibilities. Updates from upcoming conference presentations and any follow up on the February 2026 calls about the phase IIb maintenance results will help you gauge how prominent this program could become within Teva’s pipeline, next to products like the olanzapine long acting injectable. It is also worth tracking how management talks about R&D spending, debt metrics, and the balance between generics, biosimilars, and branded products as duvakitug progresses.

data.title: Pfizer’s BRAFTOVI Data Adds New Dimension To Oncology Growth Story
data.description:
• Pfizer reported positive pivotal results from its BREAKWATER trial for the BRAFTOVI based regimen in first line treatment of BRAF V600E mutant metastatic colorectal cancer.
  • The data showed significant progression free survival improvements, and Pfizer is moving the regimen through the approval process as a potential new therapy option.

For investors watching NYSE:PFE, this update adds a fresh data point to the story at a time when the stock trades around $27.37. Shares are up 6.7% over the past 30 days, 8.7% year to date, and 14.9% over the past year, while the 3 year return is a 23.2% decline. The mixed return profile helps frame how new oncology readouts can matter for sentiment around Pfizer's longer term pipeline value.

The BRAFTOVI regimen news also reinforces Pfizer's push in precision oncology, targeting a difficult colorectal cancer subset with a defined genetic mutation. If the BREAKWATER results support regulatory approval, investors may watch how quickly this potential first line option is integrated into treatment guidelines and what it could mean for the scale and durability of Pfizer's oncology portfolio over time.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Pfizer by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Pfizer.

📰 Beyond the headline: 4 risks and 1 thing going right for Pfizer that every investor should see.

The BREAKWATER readout gives Pfizer another data point in a focused, higher value segment of colorectal cancer, where targeted options for BRAF V600E-mutant disease are limited. For a company often viewed through the lens of Covid-era products and an obesity pipeline, this kind of late-stage oncology update shows how Pfizer is working to deepen its presence in specialty cancers that can support pricing and margins. The regimen still needs full data disclosure, regulatory review and eventual uptake by oncologists, but a statistically significant and clinically meaningful progression-free survival benefit in first-line use is the sort of signal many investors consider when assessing commercial potential.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Pfizer Narrative

  • The BRAFTOVI regimen aligns with the view that Pfizer is leaning into late-stage oncology and personalized therapies to support long-term growth beyond patent expirations.
  • At the same time, execution risk around converting clinical results into durable prescribing patterns could challenge the idea that pipeline progress alone offsets pressure on legacy products.
  • The specific colorectal cancer niche and potential label expansion for BRAFTOVI add clinical breadth that is not fully captured by high-level references to oncology and rare disease opportunities in the existing narrative.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Pfizer to help decide what it is worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ The BRAFTOVI regimen is still investigational in this setting, so regulatory decisions and any safety or efficacy questions could limit its eventual role in first-line treatment.
  • ⚠️ Analysts have noted that Pfizer's earnings are forecast to decline over the next few years, so even successful launches may need to work against a tougher overall earnings backdrop.
  • 🎁 Positive late-stage oncology data help support Pfizer's effort to build a broader portfolio beyond Covid-related products, which some investors see as important for sentiment and diversification.
  • 🎁 If BRAFTOVI in first-line mCRC gains approval and guideline support, it could strengthen Pfizer's footing in precision oncology alongside other large players such as Merck, Bristol Myers Squibb and Roche.

What To Watch Going Forward

Watching what comes next means tracking three threads. First, look for the full BREAKWATER Cohort 3 data at a major medical meeting, as details on safety, duration of benefit and subgroups often shape real-world uptake. Second, the FDA review process and any advisory committee feedback will help investors gauge how regulators view the benefit risk profile in this first-line setting. Third, if the regimen is approved, prescription trends, inclusion in treatment guidelines and how it competes with other targeted and chemotherapy-based options in metastatic colorectal cancer will be key signposts for potential revenue contribution.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Innodata Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company might be worth by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to today’s value using a required rate of return.

For Innodata, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, based on cash flow projections in US$. The latest twelve month free cash flow is US$39.24 million. Analysts provide an estimate of US$27.35 million for 2026, and Simply Wall St extends this with ten year projections, which gradually step down from US$27.35 million in 2026 to US$18.61 million by 2035, with each year discounted back to today.

When all those discounted cash flows are added up, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of US$12.52 per share. Compared with the recent share price of US$43.02, this implies Innodata is around 243.6% above the DCF estimate. On this model the stock screens as significantly overvalued.

Result: OVERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Innodata may be overvalued by 243.6%. Discover 56 high quality undervalued stocks or create your own screener to find better value opportunities.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Innodata.

Approach 2: Innodata Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful way to see how much you are paying for each dollar of earnings. It quickly connects the share price to what the business is currently generating in profit, which is often a primary driver of long term returns.

What counts as a “normal” P/E will vary. Higher expected growth and lower perceived risk can justify a higher multiple, while slower growth or higher risk usually points to a lower one. So you want to compare a company’s P/E to relevant benchmarks rather than in isolation.

Innodata currently trades on a P/E of 40.74x. That sits above both the Professional Services industry average of 19.43x and the peer average of 20.96x. Simply Wall St also calculates a proprietary “Fair Ratio” for Innodata of 27.35x, which is the P/E they would expect given factors such as earnings growth, industry, profit margins, market cap and company specific risks. This Fair Ratio can be more informative than a simple industry or peer comparison because it adjusts for the company’s own profile instead of assuming it should match the average. Set against the actual P/E of 40.74x, the Fair Ratio suggests Innodata’s shares are pricing in a higher level of expectations than this framework implies.

data.title: Carlton Investments (ASX:CIN) High 93.5% Margin Reinforces Earnings Heavy Narrative
data.description:
Advertisement

Carlton Investments (ASX:CIN) H1 2026 Earnings Snapshot

Carlton Investments (ASX:CIN) has posted H1 2026 results that keep its earnings story in focus, with trailing 12 month net income at A$39.5 million and basic EPS at A$1.49, set against a current share price of A$36. Looking back, the company has seen half year revenue move from A$20.1 million and EPS of A$0.72 in H2 2024 to A$21.9 million and EPS of A$0.77 in H1 2025, while the latest reported half year, H2 2025, showed revenue of A$19.7 million and EPS of A$0.70. With net profit margins sitting at very high levels over the last year, this result frames Carlton as an earnings heavy story where small shifts in profitability and dividend reliability matter a lot for investors.

See our full analysis for Carlton Investments.

With the headline numbers set, the next step is to see how this earnings profile lines up with the widely held narratives about Carlton, highlighting where the story is confirmed and where the latest figures push back.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

93.5% margins show earnings heavy model

  • Over the last 12 months, Carlton converted A$42.2 million of revenue into A$39.5 million of net income, giving it a very high 93.5% net profit margin, only slightly below the prior 93.8%.
  • What stands out for a bullish view is how this margin profile lines up with the earnings story. Five year earnings growth has averaged 10.5% per year, while the latest single year growth sits at 0.2%. Anyone arguing that high margins automatically mean strong growth has to reconcile that recent slowdown against the very rich 93.5% margin base.

10.5% five year earnings growth vs 0.2% latest year

  • Looking at the trend, reported earnings have grown at 10.5% per year over the past five years, but over the latest 12 months growth is 0.2%, which is much closer to flat than that longer term average.
  • For a more cautious take, bears often focus on this kind of gap between long term and recent growth. Here the numbers give them some support because the 10.5% multi year growth rate sits beside that 0.2% latest year figure, while half year EPS figures over 2024 and 2025 move in a fairly tight band between A$0.70 and A$0.77, which suggests recent progress has been modest even with high earnings quality flagged in the analysis.
    • Critics can point to H1 2025 net income of A$20.3 million versus H2 2025 net income of A$18.5 million as another example of how earnings have moved around a relatively narrow range instead of clearly accelerating.
    • At the same time, the fact that earnings quality is described as high means the slower 0.2% growth is not being cushioned by any mention of weaker accounting quality in the trailing data.

P/E of 24.1x with price far above DCF fair value

  • On valuation, the shares trade on a 24.1x P/E, which is lower than the peer average of 32.7x but higher than the wider Australian Capital Markets industry at 20.9x. The current A$36 share price sits well above the DCF fair value of about A$7.44.
  • What might surprise bullish investors who anchor on the P/E discount to peers is that the same data set flags the A$36 price as being far higher than the A$7.44 DCF fair value and also notes an unstable dividend record. Anyone leaning on the relative P/E alone has to weigh that against a valuation model that implies a very large premium to modelled cash flows and a dividend history that is not described as steady for income focused holders.
    • Supporters of the bullish case can reasonably highlight the five year 10.5% earnings growth and high earnings quality, yet the gap between A$36 and A$7.44 suggests the market is paying a lot compared with that specific cash flow estimate.
    • Income oriented investors also have to factor in that, alongside the P/E and DCF signals, the dividend track record is described as unstable rather than consistently growing or maintained.

While it's always nice to have higher profit, a large contribution from unusual items sometimes dampens our enthusiasm. When we crunched the numbers on thousands of publicly listed companies, we found that a boost from unusual items in a given year is often not repeated the next year.
